summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the engram HTTP server returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on all responses including CORS preflight, requires no authentication by default, and allows cross-origin POST /learn to inject arbitrary data into the knowledge graph and cross-origin GET /api/hook-log to exfiltrate project information.
expected_security_impact: Any website the user visits should be able to inject data into the knowledge graph via POST /learn and read hook log data via GET /api/hook-log, because the server sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on all responses and requires no authentication when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is not set.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the engram project from source, started the HTTP server, and confirmed: (1) Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * is returned on all API responses, (2) CORS preflight with an attacker-controlled Origin header is accepted, (3) POST /learn from an attacker origin successfully injected data into the graph (HTTP 201), (4) the injected proof string was found in graph query results, (5) GET /api/hook-log from an attacker origin returned data without authentication, (6) no ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is required by default.
verification_method: The verification script makes six sequential checks: (1) CORS wildcard header presence on API responses, (2) CORS preflight acceptance of attacker origin, (3) successful cross-origin data injection via POST /learn, (4) persistence of injected data in graph query results, (5) cross-origin data exfiltration via GET /api/hook-log, (6) absence of authentication requirement. Each check fails the reproduction if it does not pass. The proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF_CSRF_* uniquely identifies the injected data.
evidence_produced_by_run: Server returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on /health endpoint responses.
CORS preflight (OPTIONS /learn with Origin: http://evil-attacker.com) returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *.
POST /learn with attacker Origin header and JSON body {"content": "...EVOHUNT_PROOF_CSRF_..."} returns HTTP 201.
GET /query?q=EVOHUNT_PROOF_CSRF_* returns the injected proof string in graph query results.
GET /api/hook-log with attacker Origin header returns HTTP 200 with data.
GET /health with attacker Origin header succeeds without any Authorization header.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
